accommodation_options.name: Budget Hostel Shared Room
accommodation_options.description: Simple mixed-gender shared rooms with lockers, Wi-Fi, and basic breakfast included.
accommodation_options.distance_description: 10-minute tram ride from the Conservatory campus.

accommodation_options.name: City Student Dormitory – Standard
accommodation_options.description: Dormitory-style accommodation in 4-bed rooms with shared bathrooms and common kitchen.
accommodation_options.distance_description: 5 minutes on foot from the main festival venue.

accommodation_options.name: City Student Dormitory – Plus
accommodation_options.description: Refurbished dormitory wing with quieter floors and larger desks, ideal for practicing musicians.
accommodation_options.distance_description: 7-minute walk to the Conservatory and practice studios.

accommodation_options.name: Host Family Stay – Budget
accommodation_options.description: Stay with a local family, breakfast included. Shared bathroom, single bed.
accommodation_options.distance_description: Families located within 20–30 minutes by public transport.

accommodation_options.name: Host Family Stay – Premium
accommodation_options.description: Larger private room with desk and piano access in the home, two meals per day included.
accommodation_options.distance_description: Average 15 minutes door-to-door to the Conservatory.

accommodation_options.name: Shared Festival Apartment – Duplex
accommodation_options.description: Shared two-bedroom duplex apartment with kitchen and living area. Each participant has a private bedroom.
accommodation_options.distance_description: 8-minute walk to the festival shuttle stop, 12 minutes to main hall.

accommodation_options.name: Studio Apartment – Premium
accommodation_options.description: Modern studio with kitchenette, private bathroom, and digital piano on request.
accommodation_options.distance_description: Located in the city center, 15 minutes by tram to the festival sites.

accommodation_options.name: Campus Guesthouse – Single Room
accommodation_options.description: Quiet single room with private bathroom in the Conservatory guesthouse. Ideal for focused practice.
accommodation_options.distance_description: On-campus, 2-minute walk to practice rooms and main hall.

accommodation_options.name: Campus Guesthouse – Single Room (Waitlist)
accommodation_options.description: Same facilities as the standard guesthouse single; currently only waitlist spots are available.
accommodation_options.distance_description: On-campus, immediate access to teaching studios.

accommodation_options.name: Central Hotel – Single Room
accommodation_options.description: Three-star hotel room with daily breakfast and evening shuttle to main concerts.
accommodation_options.distance_description: Located in the historic center, 10–15 minutes to all main venues.

products.name: Student Full Festival Pass
products.description: Access to all standard concerts, open rehearsals, and public masterclasses for the entire festival for one registered student.

products.name: Adult Full Festival Pass
products.description: Unlimited access to all standard festival events for adults 18+.

products.name: Senior Full Festival Pass
products.description: Discounted full festival pass for attendees aged 65 and above.

products.name: Student Weekend Festival Pass
products.description: Student pass valid for all festival events from Friday evening to Sunday night.

products.name: Student Single-Day Pass
products.description: One full day of access to festival concerts and open masterclasses for students.

products.name: Workshop Add-On: Piano Pedagogy Essentials
products.description: Interactive 2-hour workshop for festival participants focusing on practical strategies for teaching intermediate piano students.

products.name: Workshop Add-On: Advanced Piano Technique Lab
products.description: Hands-on technical workshop exploring injury-preventive approaches to scales, arpeggios, and octaves.

products.name: Workshop Add-On: Classical Improvisation for Pianists
products.description: Explore stylistic improvisation in the style of Mozart, Beethoven, and Chopin.

products.name: Festival Logo T-Shirt – Black
products.description: Unisex black T-shirt with the official piano festival logo in white.

products.name: Festival Logo T-Shirt – White
products.description: Classic white T-shirt with a minimalist black piano logo.

products.name: Vintage Piano Sketch T-Shirt
products.description: Soft cotton tee featuring a hand-drawn grand piano illustration.

products.name: Kids Color Piano T-Shirt
products.description: Bright, colorful piano key design for younger festival attendees.

products.name: Festival Piano Hoodie – Navy
products.description: Warm fleece hoodie with embroidered grand piano emblem.

products.name: Ceramic Keyboard Mug
products.description: Black-and-white ceramic mug with wraparound piano keyboard print.

products.name: Canvas Tote Bag – Sheet Music
products.description: Eco-friendly tote bag printed with a classic Chopin score.

programs.title: Adult Piano Intensive – Intermediate (Week 2)
programs.description: A focused week for adult pianists at the intermediate level, including daily lessons, practice coaching, and performance opportunities.
programs.faculty_summary: Led by Prof. Elena Kovács with guest artists from major European conservatories.

programs.title: Adult Piano Intensive – Advanced (Week 2)
programs.description: High-level course for advanced pianists focusing on major concerto and sonata repertoire.
programs.faculty_summary: Masterclasses with international competition laureates and festival guest artists.

programs.title: Adult Piano Foundations – Beginner (Week 1)
programs.description: For adults beginning piano or returning after a long break, covering fundamentals of technique and reading.
programs.faculty_summary: Taught by the festival’s adult education specialists.

programs.title: Adult Chamber Music Workshop – Mixed Ensembles (Week 2)
programs.description: Perform in mixed ensembles with strings and winds; includes coaching and concert performance.
programs.faculty_summary: Coached by members of the resident festival string quartet and wind trio.

programs.title: Adult Voice Intensive – Art Song & Opera (Week 3)
programs.description: Daily lessons, language coaching, and stagecraft sessions for singers.
programs.faculty_summary: Faculty drawn from leading European opera houses.

programs.title: Youth Piano Morning Camp (Ages 10–13) – Week 1
programs.description: Morning-only piano program for motivated young players, including group lessons, supervised practice, and rhythm games.
programs.location: Youth Music Studio – Piano Room A
programs.faculty_summary: Led by youth piano specialists with assistants from the conservatory.

programs.title: Youth Strings Ensemble (Ages 10–13) – Week 1
programs.description: Orchestral and chamber music experience for young string players, with a final concert at the end of the week.
programs.faculty_summary: Coached by festival string faculty and advanced fellows.

programs.title: Junior Academy – Multi-Instrument Morning Lab (Ages 11–14) – Week 1
programs.description: Explore piano, strings, and percussion in a supportive group program with rotating faculty.
programs.faculty_summary: Team-taught by multi-instrumentalists and music educators.

programs.title: Youth Piano Performance Track (Ages 10–13) – Afternoon, Week 1
programs.description: Afternoon performance-focused track with daily stage time and repertoire coaching.

programs.title: Youth Piano Morning Camp (Ages 10–13) – Week 2
programs.description: The second-week edition of our popular youth piano morning camp, with new repertoire and activities.
programs.location: Youth Music Studio – Piano Room B
programs.faculty_summary: Rotating faculty with visiting young artist mentors.

programs.title: Junior Academy Prep (Ages 8–11) – Week 3
programs.description: Introductory program for younger musicians with group piano, singing, and rhythm activities.
programs.faculty_summary: Led by early childhood music educators.

programs.title: Adult Piano Intensive – Intermediate (Week 3)
programs.description: A continuation week for intermediate adult pianists, with a focus on Romantic repertoire.
programs.faculty_summary: Focus on Chopin, Liszt, and Schumann with daily performance classes.

venues.name: Festival Grand Hall
venues.notes: Main concert venue for evening orchestral performances and gala events.

venues.name: Conservatory Main Building
venues.notes: Houses teaching studios, practice rooms, and small recital spaces used for summer school courses.

venues.name: Chamber Music Room
venues.notes: Flexible space for chamber music rehearsals, workshops, and small concerts.

venues.name: Riverside Community Arts Center
venues.notes: Partner venue hosting outreach events and some adult education classes.

venues.name: Youth Music Studio
venues.notes: Dedicated facility for youth and junior academy programs with multiple smaller teaching rooms.

events.title: Opening Night Gala: Rachmaninoff Piano Concerto No. 2
events.subtitle: Festival Orchestra with Soloist Mira Tanaka
events.description: The official opening of the festival, featuring Rachmaninoff’s Piano Concerto No. 2 alongside orchestral favorites. A full-scale symphonic evening in the Festival Grand Hall.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Festival Orchestra conducted by Leo Baumgartner with piano soloist Mira Tanaka.
events.eligibility_notes: Open to the general public. Reduced prices for students and seniors.

events.title: Young Artists Piano Recital
events.subtitle: Emerging Festival Pianists in Recital
events.description: Hear the festival’s rising stars in an intimate evening recital featuring solo works by Bach, Chopin, Debussy, and contemporary composers.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Selected soloists from the Adult Piano Intensive and Young Artist programs.
events.eligibility_notes: Open to all; ideal for families and fellow participants.

events.title: Late-Night Jazz Piano Session
events.subtitle: Improvised Standards and Festival Jam
events.description: Wind down after the gala with a relaxed late-night jazz set featuring standards, improvisations, and spontaneous collaborations.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Festival jazz ensemble with guest pianists from the improvisation workshop.
events.eligibility_notes: Recommended for ages 16+. Bar service available with ID.

events.title: Family Piano Matinee
events.subtitle: Short Pieces for Young Ears
events.description: A one-hour, family-friendly concert of short piano pieces and interactive demonstrations designed for children and their caregivers.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Faculty and youth participants from the Junior Academy.
events.eligibility_notes: Open to families; children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Public Piano Masterclass: Beethoven Sonatas
events.subtitle: Prof. Elena Kovács Coaches Festival Participants
events.description: Observe Prof. Elena Kovács working with advanced pianists on selected Beethoven sonatas. Includes commentary on interpretation, pedaling, and articulation.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Masterclass led by Prof. Elena Kovács with selected festival pianists.
events.eligibility_notes: Free admission; seating priority given to registered festival participants.

events.title: Lunchtime Concert: Emerging Pianists
events.subtitle: Solo Mini-Recitals by Festival Participants
events.description: Bring your lunch and enjoy a relaxed midday concert showcasing promising young and adult pianists from the summer school.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Selected solo performances from the Adult Piano Intensive and Youth Piano Morning Camp.
events.eligibility_notes: Free and open to the public; no ticket required.

events.title: Lecture: Injury-Preventive Piano Technique
events.subtitle: Healthy Playing for a Lifetime
events.description: A practical lecture-demonstration on healthy technique, posture, and practice habits for pianists of all levels, with Q&A.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Presented by festival health and wellness specialist Dr. Amira Feld and pianist-educator Jonas Richter.
events.eligibility_notes: Free admission; recommended for serious students, teachers, and parents.

events.title: Youth Piano Camp Showcase
events.subtitle: Final Presentations from Week 1 Youth Piano Camp
events.description: Youth participants from the Week 1 Piano Morning Camp share short solo and ensemble pieces prepared during the week.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Youth Piano Morning Camp students coached by the Junior Academy faculty.
events.eligibility_notes: Open to families and friends of participants; limited general public seating.

events.title: Outdoor Piano Picnic Concert
events.subtitle: Light Classics on the Lawn
events.description: Enjoy light classical favorites and film music arranged for two and four hands in an informal outdoor setting. Bring a blanket and picnic.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Festival piano fellows and guest artists performing informal duos and trios.
events.eligibility_notes: Free; no reserved seating. Canceled in case of heavy rain.

events.title: Advanced Piano Masterclass: Chopin Ballades
events.subtitle: Dr. Wen Li on Narrative and Structure
events.description: An in-depth advanced masterclass focusing on Chopin’s Ballades, exploring narrative, voicing, and large-scale rubato.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Led by concert pianist and pedagogue Dr. Wen Li with selected advanced festival participants.
events.eligibility_notes: Observation open to all ticket holders; active performance slots by prior application.

events.title: Advanced Piano Masterclass: Liszt & Ravel Virtuosity
events.subtitle: Prof. Marino on Color and Control
events.description: A high-intensity session on virtuoso works by Liszt and Ravel, concentrating on coloristic pedaling and advanced finger technique.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Prof. Alessandra Marino works with competition-level pianists on Liszt and Ravel.
events.eligibility_notes: For advanced-level participants and observers. Limited active performer slots.

events.title: Advanced Piano Masterclass: Concerto Preparation
events.subtitle: Working with Orchestra and Conductor
events.description: A deep dive into preparing concerto movements, including cues, orchestral balance, and stage presence, with second piano accompaniment.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Festival conductor Leo Baumgartner and pianist Mira Tanaka coach concerto movements with advanced soloists.
events.eligibility_notes: Observation tickets available; active performers selected from the Adult Piano Intensive.

events.title: Piano Pedagogy Masterclass for Teachers
events.subtitle: Teaching Intermediate Students Effectively
events.description: A demonstration-style masterclass on teaching intermediate repertoire, including live lessons with teen students and discussion with the audience.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Led by pedagogy specialists from the festival’s teacher training team.
events.eligibility_notes: Designed for piano teachers and advanced students interested in pedagogy. Free with registration; limited public seating.

events.title: Workshop: Piano Pedagogy Essentials
events.subtitle: Practical Strategies for the Teaching Studio
events.description: An interactive workshop covering lesson planning, repertoire selection, and motivating intermediate students, with breakout discussions.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Facilitated by the festival’s pedagogy faculty with small-group activities.
events.eligibility_notes: Paid add-on workshop; recommended for piano teachers and advanced students.

events.title: Workshop: Advanced Piano Technique Lab
events.subtitle: Scales, Octaves, and Speed Without Injury
events.description: A hands-on technique lab focusing on advanced scale patterns, double notes, octaves, and endurance, with an emphasis on injury prevention.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Led by technique specialist Prof. Marina Hoff with demonstration by festival artists.
events.eligibility_notes: Paid add-on workshop for advanced pianists and teachers.

events.title: Workshop: Classical Improvisation for Pianists
events.subtitle: From Figured Bass to Fantasy
events.description: Learn tools for improvising in Classical and early Romantic styles, including patterns, cadenzas, and ornamentation.
events.performer_faculty_summary: Workshop led by improvisation specialist Dr. Rafael Ortiz.
events.eligibility_notes: Paid add-on workshop; participants should be comfortable with basic harmony.
